# Verify that `sesam init --sesam-dir <subdir>` writes git-config driver
# entries that carry the `--sesam-dir=<subdir>` flag. Git invokes those
# drivers with cwd = worktree root, so without the flag they would fail
# to find the nested `.sesam/` tree.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

mkdir sub
exec sesam init --user admin --sesam-dir sub

# Each driver line in .git/config must carry --sesam-dir=sub so the binary
# resolves the nested .sesam/ when git invokes it from the worktree root.
exec git config --local filter.sesam-filter.process
stdout '^sesam --sesam-dir=sub smudge$'

exec git config --local diff.sesam-diff.textconv
stdout '^sesam --sesam-dir=sub show$'

exec git config --local merge.sesam-merge.driver
stdout '^sesam --sesam-dir=sub audit merge %O %A %B %L %P$'

# End-to-end: commit the init, drop both the revealed README and the
# sealed blob, then check the blob back out. Removing the blob forces git
# to update it (vs a no-op when the worktree already matches HEAD), which
# is what actually triggers the smudge filter. The filter is invoked by
# git with cwd = worktree root and pathname = sub/.sesam/objects/README.md.sesam;
# it must use the --sesam-dir=sub bake-in to find the audit log and write
# the plaintext back to sub/README.md.
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

rm sub/README.md
rm sub/.sesam/objects/README.md.sesam
! exists sub/README.md
! exists sub/.sesam/objects/README.md.sesam

exec git checkout HEAD -- sub/.sesam/objects/README.md.sesam
exists sub/.sesam/objects/README.md.sesam
exec cat sub/README.md
stdout 'sesam bootstrap'
